sections.paragraph:
καταπλήσσω mid. aor. 3 sg. κατεπλήξατο (Just., A I, 56, 2). Pass.: aor. κατεπλάγην LXX; perf. pass. ptc. καταπεπληγμένοι 3 Macc 2:23 (s. πλήσσω; Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Just.; variously ‘strike down, terrify, amaze’). In a transf. sense from ‘strike down’ amaze, astound τινά someone (Philo, Omn. Prob. Lib. 100; Jos., Ant. 14, 370; Just., A I, 56, 2) IPol 3:1. Perf. pass. καταπέπληγμαι in act. sense be amazed, astounded τὶ at someth. (class.; PSI 502, 8 [III b.c.]; cp. 2 Macc 3:24; Philo, Post. Cai. 147; Jos., Vi. 120) οὗ καταπέπληγμαι τὴν ἐπιείκειαν at whose gentleness I am amazed IPhld 1:1.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

perf. perf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

transf. transf. = transferred

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

act. act. = active

someth. someth. = something

class. class. = classical

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 525
